report_name: DentaQuest Data Breach Exposes PHI of 2.6 Million; ShinyHunters Claims Attack

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A well-known cybercriminal group responsible for the DentaQuest data breach. The group claimed to have exfiltrated over 234GB of sensitive data, including PII and PHI, and listed it on their dark web forum as part of a double-extortion tactic.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through the sale and threatened leak of stolen data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor accessed and exfiltrated 234GB of sensitive data, including PII and PHI, from DentaQuest's internal databases.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters exfiltrated 234GB of data, likely using encrypted web protocols like HTTPS to blend with normal traffic and avoid detection.

attack_patterns.name: Archive Collected Data
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor likely used compression utilities such as 7-Zip to stage and password-protect the 234GB of stolen data before exfiltration.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: A legitimate data synchronization tool suggested as a likely tool used by ShinyHunters to exfiltrate large volumes of data.

tools.name: MegaCMD
tools.description: A legitimate data synchronization tool suggested as a likely tool used by ShinyHunters to exfiltrate large volumes of data.

tools.name: 7-Zip
tools.description: A file compression utility likely used by the threat actor to stage and password-protect stolen data before exfiltration, as indicated by hunting hints.

identities.name: DentaQuest
identities.description: A Massachusetts-based administrator of dental and vision benefits that suffered a data breach affecting 2.6 million individuals.

identities.name: 2.6 million DentaQuest members
identities.description: The group of individuals whose personal and health information was compromised in the DentaQuest data breach.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum where the ShinyHunters group advertised and listed the 234GB of data stolen from DentaQuest. The site is known to operate on domains such as breached.vc.

indicators.name: ShinyHunters Leak Site Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: ShinyHunters Leak Site DNS Query
id: 7c5b1a9f-8d2e-4f6a-b1c9-3e4d5f6a7b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the ShinyHunters data leak site domain 'breached.vc'.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/dentaquest-2026
  - https://www.morningstar.com/news/pr-newswire/20260605dc77523/privacy-alert-dentaquest-under-investigation-for-data-breach-affecting-26-million-records
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'breached.vc'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to the known data leak site domain used by ShinyHunters.

indicators.name: Suspicious Archive Creation with 7-Zip
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Archive Creation with 7-Zip
id: 8e9d0c1b-2a3f-4b5c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 7-Zip command line to create a password-protected archive, a TTP often used by threat actors like ShinyHunters to stage data for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/dentaquest-2026
  - https://www.morningstar.com/news/pr-newswire/20260605dc77523/privacy-alert-dentaquest-under-investigation-for-data-breach-affecting-26-million-records
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\7z.exe'
  selection_cli_1:
    CommandLine|contains: ' a '
  selection_cli_2:
    CommandLine|contains: ' -p'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of 7-Zip for archiving with passwords.
indicators.description: Detects the use of 7-Zip to create password-protected archives, a technique used for staging data before exfiltration.

indicators.name: Abuse of Data Synchronization Tools for Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Abuse of Data Synchronization Tools for Exfiltration
id: 9f0a1b2c-3d4e-5f6a-7b8c-9d0e1f2a3b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone.exe or megacmd.exe, which are frequently abused by threat actors like ShinyHunters to exfiltrate large volumes of data.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/dentaquest-2026
  - https://www.morningstar.com/news/pr-newswire/20260605dc77523/privacy-alert-dentaquest-under-investigation-for-data-breach-affecting-26-million-records
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative use of these data synchronization tools.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of rclone.exe or megacmd.exe, tools often abused for data exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Abuse of Data Synchronization Tools for Exfiltration
